verified.name: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program
verified.tagline: Real-time global earthquake feeds as GeoJSON
verified.description: The USGS FDSN event service streams earthquake catalogs going back decades, filterable by time, place, and magnitude. Ask for GeoJSON and you get tidy Features carrying location, depth, and shake metadata for every quake. No key, wide-open CORS, and it's U.S. government public-domain data.

properties.title: M 0.7 - 15 km S of Anza, CA

verified.name: USGS Water Services
verified.tagline: Live streamflow and gauge readings for US waters
verified.description: USGS Water Services exposes instantaneous and historical measurements from thousands of stream, lake, and groundwater sites across the United States. Query by site number and parameter code (like 00060 for discharge) and receive WaterML-flavored JSON. Public-domain data with no signup required.

verified.name: World Bank
verified.tagline: Development indicators for every country on Earth
verified.description: The World Bank's open data API serves thousands of development indicators — GDP, population, health, education — across countries and decades. Endpoints return paginated JSON with a metadata header followed by the data array. Free, keyless, and released under CC BY 4.0.

verified.name: FilterLists
verified.tagline: The directory of adblock and firewall filter lists
verified.description: FilterLists catalogs hundreds of filter lists used by ad blockers, firewalls, and hosts files, along with their maintainers, licenses, syntaxes, and languages. The REST endpoint returns the full catalog as JSON so you can build tooling around block-list metadata. Keyless, though it sends no CORS headers.

sampleJson.name: AdGuard Japanese filter Plus
sampleJson.description: This complements AdGuard Japanese filter and mainly consists of additional generic and specific rules…

verified.name: Microsoft Security Response Center (MSRC)
verified.tagline: Microsoft's security update documents, as CVRF JSON
verified.description: The Microsoft Security Response Center API publishes machine-readable security update summaries in the CVRF format. The updates endpoint lists every monthly release with titles, dates, and links to the full vulnerability documents. No key needed — but it quietly returns an empty body unless your client sends a browser-like User-Agent.
verified.notes: Returns an empty 200 body to non-browser User-Agents (WAF); works with a browser-like UA. No CORS headers observed.

verified.name: National Vulnerability Database
verified.tagline: The US government's CVE vulnerability database
verified.description: NIST's National Vulnerability Database offers the canonical REST feed for CVE records, complete with descriptions, CVSS scores, and references. Look up a single CVE by ID or page through the whole corpus. Keyless access is capped at five requests per 30 seconds; a free key lifts that ceiling.
verified.freeTier: Free — no key required (free key raises limits)
verified.rateLimit: 5 requests per rolling 30 seconds without an API key (50 with a free key)

verified.name: UK Police
verified.tagline: Street-level crime and policing data for England & Wales
verified.description: The data.police.uk API opens up street-level crimes, outcomes, stop-and-search records, and neighbourhood boundaries from UK police forces. This endpoint lists which months of data each force has published. Free, keyless, and released under the Open Government Licence.

verified.name: HackerNews
verified.tagline: Read every Hacker News story and comment via Firebase
verified.description: Y Combinator's Hacker News exposes its entire item graph — stories, comments, jobs, polls, and users — through a simple Firebase REST API. Fetch any item by ID to get its author, score, timestamp, and child comment IDs. No key, and it even echoes your Origin for CORS.

sampleJson.title: My YC app: Dropbox - Throw away your USB drive

verified.name: City Bikes
verified.tagline: Live bike-share stations from 500+ networks worldwide
verified.description: CityBikes aggregates real-time bike-sharing data from hundreds of systems across the globe into one consistent API. List every network with its location and operator, then drill into any network for live station availability. Free and keyless with permissive CORS.

verified.name: NHL Records and Stats
verified.tagline: Unofficial NHL franchise, records, and standings data
verified.description: The NHL's public web endpoints (records.nhl.com and api-web.nhle.com) return rich JSON on franchises, standings, schedules, and historical records. This franchise list maps every team past and present to its abbreviations and seasons. Undocumented and unofficial, but keyless and live.
verified.notes: Unofficial, undocumented NHL endpoints (records.nhl.com / api-web.nhle.com). No CORS headers observed.

verified.name: RacingHub
verified.tagline: Formula 1 history from 1950 to today, as clean JSON
verified.description: RacingHub is an open-source REST API wrapping the F1DB dataset of Formula 1 seasons, drivers, constructors, race results, and standings. Endpoints are paginated JSON — the seasons list summarizes champions and race counts year by year. Free and keyless; an independent project not affiliated with Formula 1.
verified.dataLicense: CC BY 4.0 (source data from the F1DB project)
verified.notes: Independent project, not affiliated with or endorsed by Formula 1.

verified.name: TourneyRadar
verified.description: TourneyRadar collects upcoming over-the-board chess tournaments from national federations around the world into one JSON feed. Browse events with dates, categories, locations, and geo-coordinates, or search across them. Free, keyless, and CORS-friendly, though it's a community project with no published terms.

data.name: IRT Rápido "MES DE LA MUJER" del Centro Español - 2026
data.location: Centro Español - San Martín, Santa Fe

verified.name: Bacon Ipsum
verified.description: Bacon Ipsum generates filler text seasoned with cuts of meat instead of Latin. Ask its JSON API for a number of paragraphs — all-meat or meat-and-filler — and it returns an array of savory placeholder strings. Free and keyless with open CORS.
sampleJson: Tri-tip filet mignon beef esse. Anim velit et mollit jerky ea voluptate. Quis ribeye cow consequat, capicola meatloaf in eu burgdoggen. …

verified.name: Dicebear Avatars
verified.tagline: Deterministic SVG avatars from any seed string
verified.description: DiceBear renders customizable avatars in dozens of styles from a seed you choose, so the same input always yields the same face. The /json endpoint returns the avatar as an SVG string wrapped in JSON, ready to inline. Keyless with open CORS; the sampled Pixel Art style ships under CC0.

sampleJson.svg: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 16 16" fill="none" shape-rendering="crispEdges">… pixel-art avatar for seed 'Felix' (truncated) …</svg>

verified.freeTier: Free — no key required (self-hosting advised for heavy use)
verified.dataLicense: MIT (library); avatar styles vary — sampled Pixel Art is CC0 1.0
verified.notes: Old avatars.dicebear.com is deprecated; current API is api.dicebear.com. Licenses differ per avatar style.

verified.name: FakeStoreAPI
verified.tagline: Fake products, carts, and users for e-commerce demos
verified.description: FakeStoreAPI serves realistic-looking but fictional store data — products, categories, carts, and users — for prototyping shopping UIs. Grab a single product to get its title, price, category, image, and rating. Free and keyless with open CORS.

sampleJson.title: Fjallraven - Foldsack No. 1 Backpack, Fits 15 Laptops
sampleJson.description: Your perfect pack for everyday use and walks in the forest…

skipped.name: Escape
skipped.reason: Hosted endpoint (s.polarspetroll.repl.co) no longer resolves — Replit repl.co hosting was discontinued.

skipped.name: Mozilla http scanner
skipped.reason: Observatory API requires a POST to start a scan; no keyless JSON GET (GET /scan returns 404).

skipped.name: Mozilla tls scanner
skipped.reason: tls-observatory.services.mozilla.com no longer connects — service decommissioned.

skipped.name: PhishStats
skipped.reason: Site rebuilt on Astro; documented REST endpoint /api/phishing now 404s — no keyless JSON GET found within attempts.

skipped.name: URLhaus
skipped.reason: urlhaus-api.abuse.ch requires an Auth-Key header (401 Unauthorized) — no keyless access.

skipped.name: Fuck Off as a Service
skipped.reason: Profanity-as-a-service — skipped as NSFW per policy.

skipped.name: Hashnode
skipped.reason: GraphQL-only API (POST to /graphql) — no keyless JSON GET endpoint.

skipped.name: Open Collective
skipped.reason: GraphQL-only API (POST to /graphql) — no keyless JSON GET endpoint.

skipped.name: NBA Stats
skipped.reason: stats.nba.com does not respond to server-side requests (connection times out) and requires browser-specific headers.
